channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.130 (Insiders)
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.130 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Hybrid Logic Apps Deployment on Red Hat OpenShift
item.description: What makes OpenShift different? If you've done this on K3s or AKS, most of the flow is identical as mentioned in Set up your own infrastructure for Standard logic app workflows - Azure Logic Apps | Microsoft Learn. Two OpenShift specifics are worth flagging up front. First, SCCs: the default...
item.dc:creator: anandgmenon

item.title: AI credit pools for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description: You can now manage a cost center’s AI credit pool directly in the billing UI where you create and edit cost centers. Previously, you could only manage this through the… The post AI credit pools for cost centers in the billing UI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: T-SQL Hygiene: Introducing the Covering Index
item.description: Often in applications, we write database queries. And often, the same query is executed again and again. To make queries against large tables faster, we can add an index. This is a general improvement for anyone accessing the table. However, there is a specially designed index called a covering...
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: $100 million for open source: A milestone built by the community
item.description: Celebrating $100 million contributed by the community to the people who build and sustain open source every day. The post $100 million for open source: A milestone built by the community appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Lincoln

item.title: Introducing Azure Front Door edge actions - Bringing secure, programmable logic to the edge
item.description: Modern web applications are expected to feel instant, secure, and personalized, no matter where users connect from or what device they use. Delivering that experience requires more than moving static content closer to users. It increasingly depends on making smart decisions at the edge, from...
item.dc:creator: akhilkarmalkar

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Protect sensitive generative AI telemetry in Application Insights and Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Azure Monitor Application Insights now stores generative AI content in a dedicated GenAIContent table (AppGenAIContent in Log Analytics), making it possible to apply newly available access controls to sensitive AI telemetry. Because Application Insights p
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Automating Oracle to SQL Migration Using SSMA Console Skill in GitHub Copilot
item.description: Introduction Enterprise scale Oracle-to-SQL migrations often struggle with inconsistent execution, manual effort, and lack of repeatability across environments. These challenges slow down delivery, introduce risk, and limit the ability to scale migration programs effectively. The SSMA Console Skill...
item.dc:creator: KapilSamant

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway
item.description: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway enables customers to run IPv6 inner traffic through VPN tunnels in a dual‑stack configuration. IPv6 is supported on all production SKU gateways that use standard public IPs.IPv6 inner traffic can be configured for both n
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: AI agents, meet the Azure Cosmos DB vNext emulator
item.description: If you use the Azure Cosmos DB vNext emulator, you probably know the local development loop: start the emulator, connect to it, create some resources, load test data, run queries, and inspect the results. Each step is straightforward, but together they add setup work before you can test the...
item.dc:creator: Abhishek Gupta

item.title: GitHub Code Quality is now generally available
item.description: GitHub Code Quality is now generally available on GitHub Enterprise Cloud and GitHub Team. It solves an emerging challenge for software development: AI accelerates code output, and Code Quality helps… The post GitHub Code Quality is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Join us for our MCP Live! — A free livestream covering all things MCP
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is the open standard for connecting AI models to tools and data. It was first introduced to the world by Anthropic in November 2024, and is now the most widely adopted standard in the world of Generative AI. You can now use MCP servers to connect agents to your data...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Round Table: Building Browser-Capable Agents with the Browser Automation Tool
item.description: Some of the most valuable work still lives inside a browser: booking a class, pulling a figure off a dashboard, filling in a portal form, gathering research across a dozen tabs. These are exactly the tasks people wish an agent could just do. On 22 July 2026 at 2:30 PM BST (7:00 PM IST), the...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Meet the IQ's: How Microsoft is Creating Context-Aware AI
item.description: Microsoft Architect's: Lavanya Sreedhar LavanyaSreedhar​, Tom Dinh Tom-Dinh​, Oviya Soundararajan oviyasound​, and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ The AI era demands more than powerful language models. It demands context a deep understanding of what enterprise data means, how it connects, and how AI systems...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Repository-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics generally available
item.description: The Copilot usage metrics REST API now reports repository-level activity. Two new endpoints return a daily, per-repository breakdown of pull request activity for Copilot coding agent and Copilot code review.… The post Repository-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics generally available appeared first...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot app now available in the usage metrics API
item.description: The Copilot usage metrics API now reports the GitHub Copilot app usage in the enterprise and organization 1-day and 28-day reports. This gives enterprise and organization admins visibility into the… The post GitHub Copilot app now available in the usage metrics API appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot code review: Customization and configurability improvements
item.description: Copilot code review now utilizes a firewall, custom setup steps, and independent runner configurations. It now reads custom instructions from the head branch to allow for easy testing and validation… The post Copilot code review: Customization and configurability improvements appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Fix pull request comments with Copilot cloud agent
item.description: You can now select Fix with Copilot directly from Copilot code review pull request comments in GitHub Mobile. The button is available both on the pull request’s main view and… The post GitHub Mobile: Fix pull request comments with Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Python 3.14
item.description: You can now develop functions using Python 3.14 locally and deploy them to Azure Functions plans on Linux. Upgrade your apps to Python 3.14 to take advantage of enhanced security, a longer support window, and continued compatibility with the Azure Functio
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Functions Support for PowerShell 7.6
item.description: Azure Functions support for PowerShell 7.6 is now available in preview. You can now develop apps using PowerShell 7.6 locally and deploy them to Azure Functions plans. Learn more: What's new in PowerShell 7.6 Azure Functions PowerShell versions and their
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Microsoft Foundry Now Has an AI Gateway Control Plane — What Changes for App Service
item.description: In May, I published a runnable sample that put Azure API Management in front of an AI agent on Azure App Service. APIM handled token limits, semantic caching, token metrics, and access to the model. The point was simple: keep the agent framework interchangeable and put the production controls at...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: The cost of saying yes has changed
item.description: The cost of writing code dropped; the cost of owning it didn't. A framework for deciding which changes are actually cheap in the AI era. The post The cost of saying yes has changed appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Dalia Abuadas

item.title: Microsoft at Black Hat USA 2026: Defending trust in the age of AI and supply chain attacks
item.description: Join Microsoft Security at Black Hat USA 2026 for supply chain research, hands-on security experiences, expert conversations, and our reception. The post Microsoft at Black Hat USA 2026: Defending trust in the age of AI and supply chain attacks appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Elliot Volkman

item.title: Hypervelocity Engineering: Accelerating Enterprise AI with Azure AI Landing Zones
item.description: Artificial Intelligence is evolving at unprecedented speed. The challenge for enterprises is no longer building AI solutions—it is engineering AI platforms that can adapt, scale, and govern innovation continuously. Hypervelocity Engineering (HVE) provides the engineering operating model that...
item.dc:creator: VimalVerma

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: Microsoft Defender Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server are now generally available. You can use assessments to continuously evaluate the security posture of your Azure PostgreSQL flexible
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Encryption in Transit for Azure Files NFS Shares in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now generally supports Encryption in Transit (EiT) for Azure Files NFS v4.1 volumes through the Azure File CSI driver. With EiT enabled, data transferred between AKS workloads and Azure Files NFS shares is encrypted using TL
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How to test agent skills without hitting real APIs
item.description: Your agent skill calls an API. The moment you start evaluating it, every run either costs money or mutates production data. Learn how to mock APIs transparently so you can run evals without changing your skill or hitting real endpoints.
item.dc:creator: Waldek Mastykarz

item.title: Teaching a Vision Model to See Like a Human Annotator—and Catching It When It Lies
item.description: A multimodal LLM enrichment pipeline that extracts structured metadata from visual assets, constrains output to predefined values to minimize hallucinations, and uses a ground truth evaluation template to measure quality—all as a plug-and-play module. The post Teaching a Vision Model to See Like a...
item.dc:creator: Deeptanil Saha, Kartheek Palepu

item.title: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity
item.description: From late April 2026 to mid-June 2026, Microsoft Defender Experts observed increased ACR Stealer activity across customer environments. These campaigns are successfully using ClickFix lures to steal browser credentials, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents from enterprise environments....
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Security Research and Balaji Venkatesh S

item.title: Azure Cobalt: Workload-Aware Power Management for More Efficient Datacenters
item.description: As cloud services and AI workloads continue to grow, power has become a key constraint in expanding compute capacity. Using available power more efficiently enables Azure to deploy more servers in each datacenter, helping meet growing customer demand while improving sustainability. To address this...
item.dc:creator: redsa

item.title: Building Agents in Production with Toolbox, Skills, and Tool Search
item.description: If you are shipping AI agents beyond a demo, you have felt the pain: every agent needs the same tools, each with its own authentication, and the tool list keeps growing until your prompt is bloated and the model picks the wrong one. On 22 July 2026 at 5:00 PM BST, the Microsoft Foundry community is...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: (Co)work in Progress
item.description: A complete record of milestones, launches, and newest innovations. Introducing event-triggered tasks in Cowork: delegate the watching, not just the work Scheduled tasks handle the predictable. But most of what matters at work doesn't run on a clock — it just happens: your manager pings you about a...
item.dc:creator: ilyagrebnov

item.title: Advanced search for Projects is generally available
item.description: The filter bar in GitHub Projects views now supports advanced search, so you can build the exact view you need with logical AND and OR instead of maintaining a separate… The post Advanced search for Projects is generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Repository admins can archive pull requests
item.description: Repository admins can now archive pull requests to remove them from public view without permanently deleting them. When a pull request is archived, it is closed and locked. Pull requests… The post Repository admins can archive pull requests appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: REST API endpoints for Visual Studio Subscription management
item.description: GitHub Enterprise Cloud admins can now use the following REST API endpoints to programmatically manage Visual Studio Subscription (VSS) assignments: GET /enterprises/{enterprise}/visual-studio-subscriptions: Returns all VSS assignments for an enterprise, including… The post REST API endpoints for...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Xcode 27 runner image now in public preview
item.description: You can now build and test your Apple applications against Xcode 27 on GitHub-hosted macOS runners. This is now available in public preview. With early access to the latest Xcode… The post Xcode 27 runner image now in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: T-SQL Hygiene: What’s the Big Deal with SET NOCOUNT ON?
item.description: Learn why SET NOCOUNT ON improves stored procedure efficiency, reduces unnecessary messages, and matters to .NET developers. The post T-SQL Hygiene: What’s the Big Deal with SET NOCOUNT ON? appeared first on Azure SQL Dev Corner.
item.dc:creator: Jerry Nixon

item.title: Announcing .NET Modernization for Beginners
item.description: A free, open-source, hands-on course that walks you through modernizing a real legacy ASP.NET application all the way to .NET 10 using the GitHub Copilot modernization agent, step by step. The post Announcing .NET Modernization for Beginners appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Pablo Lopes

item.title: Join Us Live: Understanding Copilot Cowork and Managing Copilot Credits
item.description: The way we work with AI is evolving rapidly, and so is the way organizations manage and scale those experiences. If you're exploring Copilot Cowork, navigating Copilot Credits, or looking for guidance on consumption-based AI experiences, you won't want to miss our upcoming live event. 🎙️ Live AMA:...
item.dc:creator: JonJones_MSFT

item.title: Announcing Public Preview of Autoscale for Azure Event Grid Namespaces
item.description: Today we are pleased to announce the public preview of Autoscale for Azure Event Grid namespaces. With this capability, an Event Grid namespace in the Standard tier automatically adjusts its throughput unit (TU) allocation in response to real-time workload utilization. This removes the need to...
item.dc:creator: Pritha_Das

item.title: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description: As AI agents become more autonomous, strong identity, access, and auditing controls are critical to keeping them secure. The post Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Yesenia Yser and Toby Kohlenberg

item.title: Agents can learn with Memory in Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
item.description: Recurring Failure We are entrusting AI agents with an increasing range of tasks, from preparing briefs to scheduling meetings, and from filing tax returns to building and debugging production systems. While their capabilities continue to improve rapidly, the experience remains frustrating in a...
item.dc:creator: jianant

item.title: How to build long-running MCP tools on Azure Functions
item.description: Learn how to build long-running MCP tools on Azure Functions using Durable Functions. This post explains why synchronous tool calls break down for long-running work, introduces the MCP Tasks extension, and walks through a sample pattern that works with existing request/response clients today. The...
item.dc:creator: Lily Ma

item.title: MCP Enterprise Authorization Is Here — What Entra and App Service Can Do Today
item.description: A few weeks ago I wrote about what the latest MCP changes meant for scaling on Azure App Service. This time, the protocol change is about a different kind of scale: how an enterprise connects hundreds or thousands of employees to MCP servers without making every person authorize every server one at...
item.dc:creator: jordanselig

item.title: Visual Studio Administrator? Join our Private Marketplace Preview!
item.description: Organizations are increasingly looking for greater control over extensions within development environments. Driven by security, compliance, and internal governance requirements, teams want more visibility into how developers discover and acquire extensions. To address these needs, we’re excited to...
item.dc:creator: Ruben Rios

item.title: Choose the right speech and voice model
item.description: Choose the right speech and voice model in Microsoft Foundry The practical approach to selecting the right model is simple: do not ask which speech model is best. Ask what the speech system must guarantee. Once you know whether the job is conversation, translation, transcription, diarization,...
item.dc:creator: Anil_Korkut

item.title: Understanding billing for the Azure Copilot Observability Agent
item.description: The Azure Copilot Observability Agent brings an agentic investigation experience directly into Azure Monitor. Teams can chat with their observability data, run deep investigations across application and infrastructure signals, and, in preview, use autonomous operations to correlate alerts and...
item.dc:creator: Noa Kuperberg

item.title: How Microsoft 365 built a platform engineering layer on AKS to ship faster at global scale
item.description: Challenge: Scaling success without increasing complexity Microsoft 365 runs a broad portfolio of services across global and sovereign clouds. At that scale, the platform has to do more than schedule workloads. It has to enforce security boundaries, meet enterprise compliance requirements, and...
item.dc:creator: Suma SaganeGowda

item.title: Streaming and Batch Data Architectures with Microsoft Fabric to Azure Databricks
item.description: Author's: Aladdin Alchalabi aalchalabi​, Oscar Alvarado oscaralvarado​ and Rafia Aqil Rafia_Aqil​ Note: This article describes a solution idea. Your cloud architect can use this guidance to help visualize the major components for a typical implementation. Use this article as a starting point to...
item.dc:creator: Rafia_Aqil

item.title: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
item.description: Threat actors compromised AsyncAPI packages and weaponized trusted CI/CD workflows to distribute malware through npm. This analysis breaks down the attack chain, payload delivery, and recommended defenses. The post Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Security Research, Ravikant Tiwari, Sagar Patil, Suriyaraj Natarajan and Arvind Gowda
